task_completion.description: Verify that 'Sales', 'Profit', and 'Customer Name' are selected for the visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The grammar pane or visualization setup shows 'Sales' and 'Profit' assigned to the axes (e.g., X-axis and Y-axis) and 'Customer Name' assigned to the detail, category, or points section.

task_completion.description: Verify that a scatter plot is successfully rendered on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A high-density scatter plot with many individual data points (dots) is visible on the main Oracle Analytics Desktop canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that clustering analytics has been applied to the scatter plot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Analytics pane shows 'Clusters' added to the visualization, or the grammar pane shows a 'Cluster' pill applied to the Color section.

task_completion.description: Verify that exactly 5 customer segments (clusters) are identified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scatter plot data points are partitioned into exactly 5 distinct colors, and the chart legend explicitly lists 5 cluster groups (e.g., Cluster 1 through Cluster 5).

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is saved as 'Customer_Segmentation.dva' in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog shows the name 'Customer_Segmentation' and the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\', OR the application title bar displays 'Customer_Segmentation' and a file explorer confirms the .dva file's location.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface (drag-and-drop, clicking menus, using the Analytics pane) rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the visualization uses the actual dataset and is not a mocked image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data pane displays the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset, and the scatter plot shows a realistic, high-density distribution of points corresponding to thousands of rows, rather than a sparse or generic placeholder chart.

integrity.description: Verify the file was saved using the application's native functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The native Oracle Analytics Desktop 'Save Workbook' dialog is used, rather than a terminal command creating or moving a file into the directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 2,683 unique customers, so the scatter plot should be high-density with many individual data points representing Sales vs Profit. The clustering must be configured to exactly 5 groups, visually represented by 5 distinct colors and 5 legend items. The final workbook must be saved as 'Customer_Segmentation.dva' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\'.
